stem: An engineer takes 15 measurements of fuel level using a fuel gauge. The measurements have a mean of 102.3 % and a standard deviation of 7.0 %. However, calibration tests reveal that a fuel gauge systematically overestimates the true value by between 1.2% and 3.9%. Estimate the true fuel level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 102.3 %. After correcting for the 2.5% bias: ≈ 99.8 %. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (1.2%-3.9%) and sampling variability: [94.9, 104.7]. Point estimate: 99.8 %.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
